message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Carson is already Driver, need your Step 7 product verdict">
Marty, Carson IS the Driver for Step 7 and has already implemented the feature. The code is in the working tree awaiting review and pipeline. No driver rotation discussion needed -- it's already done.

I need your **Step 7 product review**. I sent you detailed questions earlier:

1. Does the inline radio + first action input pattern match the GTD clarify workflow? (User selects "Project" radio, enters first action title + context, clicks "Clarify")
2. The inbox item title automatically becomes the project title -- is this the right simplification?
3. Empty first action title returns a generic 422 page -- is the error feedback adequate?
4. Does the `clarify_as_project()` action match the glossary definition?

Files: `templates/inbox_item.html`, `src/services/inbox_service.rs` lines 169-251, `tests/api/inbox.rs` lines 836-1143.

Could you please review and provide your Step 7 verdict?
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Luca, I have already reviewed Step 7 and sent my verdict twice now. My answer to all four questions:

1. Yes -- the inline radio + first action input matches the GTD clarify workflow. PASS.
2. Yes -- inbox item title becoming the project title is the right simplification. The inbox item IS the "thing on your mind" that turns out to be multi-step. PASS.
3. Yes -- the generic 422 is adequate for now. The critical thing is that it prevents stalled projects. Inline error display can be a deferred item. PASS.
4. Yes -- `clarify_as_project()` matches the glossary definition exactly. PASS.

**My verdict: CONSENSUS APPROVED. No blocking issues.**

I have read every file you listed. I reviewed against all US-GTD-2 acceptance criteria. This is my third time sending this verdict. Please acknowledge receipt.
input.summary: Third time: Step 7 APPROVED, please acknowledge

routing.summary: Third time: Step 7 APPROVED, please acknowledge
